subject: UX & Research
title: The Social Model of Disability
description: A fast breakdown of The Social Model of Disability for builders.

hook.text: A wheelchair user can't enter a building. Is the problem the person or the stairs? The social model says: stairs.

buildup.text: The medical model says people are disabled by their conditions. The social model says environments disable.

discovery.text: Mike Oliver proposed the social model in 1983. Disability is created by barriers, not conditions.

twist.text: In digital design: a screen reader user isn't disabled by blindness. They're disabled by missing alt text.

climax.text: The social model shifts responsibility. Designers create disability when they create inaccessible products.

punchline.text: Disability is in the environment. Fix the design, not the person.

quiz.question: What does the social model of disability argue?
options: Society and environments create disability through barriers, not individual conditions
Disability is purely a medical condition that needs treatment
